raw_consumer_statement:
A ticket vending company charged my American Express card over {$2000.00} for a purchase that I never made. I received an email alert from American Express because the purchase was so large, and within minutes I contacted the ticket vendor to alert them of the fraudulent charge. The vendor acknowledged that I had not made the charge but stated that it was unable to cancel it at this stage, a mere 18 minutes after it was purportedly placed. The vendor suggested I contact American Express to halt the charge ( which still showed as " pending '' ), which I immediately did. Despite the vendor having advised that they were unable to cancel the fraudulent charge when I contacted them within 10 minutes of it appearing, the American Express agent stated that they also could not stop the still " pending '' charge made less than 30 minutes prior, and that the charge can be canceled from the merchants end only. So, rather than blocking the still pending charge, American Express simply marked the charge for monitoring '' and instructed me to dispute it when it finally posted. I did so, but American Express baseless sustained the charge after shuffling me back and forth between their customer service disputes department ( which claimed it was a fraud issue ) and their fraud department ( which nonsensically claimed it could not be treated as fraud because I had done business with this vendor in the past, and therefore was a a disputes department issue ). 

On XX/XX/year>, I resubmitted the dispute to American Express with a letter detailing the series of events and providing all relevant attachments ( Exhibit A ). On XX/XX/year>, at XXXX XXXX., I received an email from American Express acknowledging the re-dispute and stating that " the amount of {$2000.00} has been placed on hold and you will not be responsible for this while our investigation is in progress. '' ( Exhibit B ). XXXX minutes later, I received another email from American Express stating : " During our investigation, we determined that this case should be handled by our Fraud department. As soon as they have reviewed your account, they will contact you directly. At the beginning of this investigation, we removed the amount ( s ) under review of {$2000.00} from your account. Since this concludes the Customer Service investigation, we have reapplied the amount under review to your account, which will reflect on your upcoming statement. '' ( Exhibit C ). American Express then re-billed the fraudulent charge, but I was never contacted by the Fraud department as promised, and have not received any evidence that the fraud department even conducted any investigation at all. 

American Express is acting illegally and in bad faith by refusing to reverse this plainly fraudulent charge.
